title: 10 best herbal creams for cold sore

remedy_properties: Soothing Anti-Inflammatory Compounds: Herbal aloe creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like aloin, which help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with cold sores by soothing the affected area.

Antiviral Activity: Aloe vera contains antiviral compounds that can inhibit the replication of the herpes simplex virus (HSV), which causes cold sores, helping to prevent further spread and promote healing.

Hydrating Properties: Herbal aloe creams retain moisture and provide hydration to the skin, which helps to accelerate the healing process by reducing dryness and cracking associated with cold sore outbreaks.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera, also known as aloe, creams helps with cold sore because of its anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. The gel inside the aloe vera leaf contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which have been shown to reduce swelling and pain associated with cold sores. Additionally, aloe vera's soothing and moisturizing effects can help to accelerate the healing process, reducing the duration and severity of cold sore outbreaks. Its natural antiviral properties also help to combat the herpes simplex virus that causes cold sores.
remedy_constituents: Aloe-emodin: Aloe-emodin, a type of anthraquinone, has been shown to inhibit the replication of herpes simplex virus, the primary cause of cold sores, and reduce the severity of symptoms.
Glucomannan: Glucomannan, a polysaccharide, has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with cold sores.

Vitamins and minerals (especially Vitamin A, Vitamin E, and Zinc): These vitamins and minerals have antioxidant properties that help to soothe and heal the skin, reducing the duration and severity of cold sore outbreaks.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make creams for cold sores due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Gel from Leaves: The clear gel inside the leaves is used to make creams for cold sores due to its ability to reduce redness and promote healing.
Aloe vera Polysaccharides from Leaves: These are used to make creams for cold sores as they have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce pain and swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of aloe vera gel from mature leaves within 24 hours of collection.
Grind 50g of beeswax and 25g of coconut oil into fine powders using a spice grinder.
Combine 100g of aloe vera gel with 50g of distilled water and 5g of vitamin e oil in a stainless steel bowl.
Heat the mixture of aloe vera gel and distilled water to 60 degrees celsius over 10 minutes.
Add the ground beeswax and coconut oil mixture to the aloe vera gel mixture and stir until it thickens over 15 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Pot marigold's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, help reduce swelling and redness associated with cold sore outbreaks.
Antiviral properties: The cream's antiviral properties, including its ability to inhibit the replication of herpes simplex virus (HSV), can help shorten the duration and severity of cold sores.
Wound-healing properties: Pot marigold's antioxidant and antibacterial compounds promote faster wound healing by reducing oxidative stress, preventing bacterial growth, and improving skin integrity.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis, also known as pot marigold, creams helps with cold sore because of its anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. The active compounds present in Calendula, such as triterpenoids and carotenoids, have been shown to reduce redness and swelling associated with cold sores. Additionally, its antiviral properties may help combat the herpes simplex virus, which causes cold sores. By applying Calendula cream to the affected area, it may aid in healing the cold sore more quickly and reduce the duration of its appearance.
remedy_constituents: Phenolics: Phenolics in Calendula officinalis creams have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce redness and swelling associated with cold sores, promoting faster healing.

Sesquiterpenes: Sesquiterpenes in Calendula officinalis creams have antimicrobial properties, which help combat the herpes simplex virus that causes cold sores, reducing the severity and duration of outbreaks.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Calendula officinalis creams have antioxidant properties, which help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, promoting faster healing and reducing the risk of scarring associated with cold sores.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are rich in anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to soothe and heal cold sore symptoms.
Leaves: They contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that aid in reducing redness and promoting wound healing.
Seeds: While not as commonly used, some products may include calendula seeds for their potential anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 dried calendula officinalis flowers and finely chop them into small pieces.
Combine the chopped calendula flowers with 8 ounces of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 6-8 hours or until it reaches a temperature of 180-200 degrees fahrenheit.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean bowl and discard the solids.
Mix the infused oil with 2 ounces of beeswax and 2 ounces of cocoa butter to create a smooth cream.

remedy_properties: Antiviral compounds: Tea tree oil contains compounds like terpinen-4-ol and alpha-terpineol, which have been shown to inhibit the replication of herpes simplex virus (HSV), the virus that causes cold sores.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The menthol and camphor present in tea tree oil can help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with cold sores, providing relief from discomfort.
Antimicrobial activity: Tea tree oil's antimicrobial properties can help prevent bacterial and fungal infections that may exacerbate or prolong the healing process of cold sores.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia, also known as tea tree, creams helps with cold sore because of its potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The tea tree oil present in these creams has been shown to reduce the severity and duration of cold sore outbreaks. Its ability to combat viral infections, such as the herpes simplex virus that causes cold sores, helps to promote faster healing and reduce the risk of future outbreaks. Additionally, tea tree oil's anti-inflammatory properties can help to soothe and calm the affected area.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): This terpene helps to reduce inflammation and ease pain associated with cold sores.
Caryophyllene: This sesquiterpene exhibits antimicrobial properties, which can help to combat the herpes simplex virus responsible for cold sores.
Linalool: This terpene possesses anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which can help to reduce the severity and duration of cold sore outbreaks.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Melaleuca alternifolia are used due to their high content of eucalyptol, a compound that has antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties.
Buds: The buds of Melaleuca alternifolia are used due to their concentration of eucalyptol, which can help in reducing the severity and duration of cold sore outbreaks.
Stems: The stems of Melaleuca alternifolia are used due to their eucalyptol content, which can aid in healing and soothing cold sore symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather and dry 500 grams of melaleuca alternifolia leaves with a high essential oil content for 7 days.
Steep 250 grams of the dried leaves in 1 liter of carrier oil at 60 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Strain the infused oil using cheesecloth and discard the solids after 30 minutes of pressure.
Mix 200 milliliters of the infused oil with 200 grams of beeswax and 100 milliliters of distilled water.
Whip the mixture at room temperature using a hand mixer for 5 minutes until it thickens.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Echinacea contains compounds that reduce inflammation and swelling, which can help alleviate discomfort and pain associated with cold sores.
Antiviral properties: Echinacea has been shown to exhibit antiviral activity against herpes simplex virus (HSV), which is the cause of cold sores.

Immunomodulatory properties: Echinacea extracts have been found to stimulate immune cells, such as natural killer cells and macrophages, which can help boost the body's defenses against viral infections.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Echinacea angustifolia, also known as Kansas coneflower, creams helps with cold sore because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. The cream's active compounds, such as alkylamides and polyacetylenes, have been shown to reduce inflammation, ease pain, and accelerate the healing process of cold sores. Additionally, Echinacea angustifolia's immunomodulatory effects can help boost the body's natural defenses against the herpes simplex virus, which causes cold sores, thereby reducing their frequency and severity. This natural remedy provides a safe and effective way to manage cold sore outbreaks.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce the severity and duration of cold sore symptoms by inhibiting the growth of the herpes simplex virus.

Phenylethanoids: Phenylethanoids have been shown to possess antiviral and immunomodulatory properties, which can help stimulate the immune system to fight off the herpes simplex virus and reduce the frequency and severity of cold sore outbreaks.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Echinacea angustifolia have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with cold sore outbreaks, promoting faster healing and recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the most commonly used part of Echinacea angustifolia due to their high concentration of alkaloids, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
Leaves: The leaves are also used due to their content of phenolic compounds, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and heal cold sore symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather 15 grams of dried echinacea angustifolia root and 30 grams of beeswax for the base of the cream.
Infuse 250 ml of carrier oil with the dried herb in a double boiler for 4 hours at 150 degrees fahrenheit.
Combine the infused oil with 50 grams of shea butter and 30 grams of beeswax in a heat-proof mixing bowl.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler for 20 minutes or until the beeswax and shea butter melt completely.
Strain the mixture into a clean glass jar and allow it to cool before storing in a cool dark place.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, help reduce swelling and redness associated with cold sores, providing relief from discomfort and pain.

Antiviral property: Chamomile contains flavonoids and terpenoids that exhibit antiviral activity against the herpes simplex virus (HSV), which causes cold sores, helping to suppress viral replication and promote healing.

Soothing and calming property: The glycosides present in chamomile have a soothing effect on mucous membranes, reducing itching, burning, and discomfort associated with cold sores, allowing for faster recovery.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Matricaria chamomilla, also known as chamomile, creams helps with cold sore because of its anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. The soothing and calming effect of chamomile reduces redness, swelling, and pain associated with cold sores. The cream's ability to inhibit the replication of the herpes simplex virus also helps to shorten the duration and severity of the outbreak. Additionally, chamomile's antioxidant properties promote wound healing and tissue repair, making it a popular natural remedy for cold sore relief. This can provide quick and effective relief.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: This flavonoid helps with cold sore by reducing inflammation and acting as an antiviral agent, thereby inhibiting the replication of the herpes simplex virus that causes cold sores.
Chamazulene: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing the discomfort and pain associated with cold sores.
Alpha-bisabolol: This sesquiterpene has potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to accelerate the healing process of cold sores and prevent infections.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are used to make creams for cold sores because of their anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties.
Leaves: The leaves are used to make creams for cold sores due to their antioxidant and soothing effects.
Seeds: The seeds are used to make creams for cold sores because they contain chamazulene, which has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers at dawn when flowers are in full bloom.
Infuse 200 milliliters of glycerin in 1 liter of distilled water at 60 degrees celsius for 30 minutes.
Steep 20 grams of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers in 200 milliliters of distilled water for 10 minutes.
Mix the infused glycerin solution with the cooled chamomile infusion and add 50 grams of beeswax.
Filter and whip the mixture until it thickens into a smooth and creamy texture taking about 10 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: English lavender contains anti-inflammatory compounds like linalool and linalyl acetate, which help reduce swelling and ease discomfort associated with cold sore lesions.

Antiviral properties: The antiviral properties of lavender may help inhibit the replication of the herpes simplex virus (HSV), which causes cold sores, reducing the severity and duration of the outbreak.

Soothing and calming effects: Lavender's calming and soothing properties can help reduce stress and anxiety, common triggers for cold sore outbreaks, while also providing a gentle, non-irritating relief from discomfort and itching.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia, also known as English lavender, creams helps with cold sore because of its anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. The cream's active compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, reduce redness and swelling of the affected area. They also inhibit the replication of the herpes simplex virus, which causes cold sores. This leads to a faster healing process and reduced pain, making English lavender creams a popular natural remedy for managing cold sore outbreaks and promoting overall skin health.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce the severity and duration of cold sore outbreaks.
Lavandulifolioside: This phenolic glycoside has potent antiviral properties, which can help inhibit the replication of herpes simplex virus (HSV) and alleviate cold sore symptoms.
Limonene: This terpene has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and prevent the spread of HSV, thereby reducing the severity of cold sore outbreaks.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Lavandula angustifolia leaves contain compounds with antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate cold sore symptoms.
Flowers: Lavender flowers are rich in essential oils, particularly linalool and linalyl acetate, which have anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and soothing effects that can help calm cold sore outbreaks.

Seeds: Lavandula angustifolia seeds contain flavonoids and other compounds with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and promote healing in cold sore lesions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers and dry them for 2 weeks in a warm dark place.
Infuse 1/4 cup of dried flowers in 2 cups of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for 4 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth and discard the solids then transfer the oil to a clean container.
Mix 1/2 cup of the infused oil with 1/4 cup of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of cocoa butter in a double boiler.
Whip the mixture until it thickens then transfer it to a tin and label it for storage.

remedy_properties: Antiviral compounds: Peppermint creams contain antiviral compounds like menthol and menthone, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of herpes simplex virus (HSV), reducing the severity and duration of cold sore outbreaks.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Peppermint's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with cold sores by minimizing inflammation and promoting healing.

Antimicrobial activity: The antimicrobial properties of peppermint cream, particularly its ability to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi, can help prevent secondary infections that may occur during a cold sore outbreak, allowing for faster recovery.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, creams helps with cold sore because of its antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties. The menthol content in peppermint creams creates a cooling sensation on the affected area, reducing pain and discomfort. This helps to speed up the healing process and alleviate symptoms associated with cold sores, such as redness and swelling. Additionally, the antiseptic properties of peppermint help to prevent bacterial infections and promote a healthy environment for the skin to heal.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: As a potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compound, rosmarinic acid helps reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with cold sores.
Menthol: With its anesthetic and anti-inflammatory properties, menthol helps numb the affected area, reducing the discomfort and pain of cold sores.
Limonene: As a monoterpene with antimicrobial properties, limonene helps combat the Herpes simplex virus responsible for cold sores, reducing the severity and duration of outbreaks.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in menthol and menthone, which have antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and heal cold sore symptoms.
Stems: They contain compounds that help reduce pain and inflammation, making them a useful addition to creams for cold sore treatment.
Roots: They have been traditionally used to treat various skin conditions, including cold sores, due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh mentha x piperita leaves in the early morning to ensure optimal oil content.
Steep the harvested leaves in 2 cups of boiling water for 10 minutes to create a strong infusion.
Strain the infusion through cheesecloth into a clean container to remove the solid plant material.
Mix 1/2 cup of beeswax with 1/4 cup of shea butter in a double boiler to create a smooth base.
Combine the infused water with the melted wax mixture and whip until a smooth and creamy texture forms.

remedy_properties: Antiviral compounds: Herbal garlic creams contain antiviral compounds like allicin, which helps to combat the herpes simplex virus (HSV) that causes cold sores, reducing the severity and duration of outbreaks.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Garlic has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling, redness, and pain associated with cold sore lesions, providing relief from discomfort and promoting healing.

Antibacterial activity: The antibacterial compounds in herbal garlic creams, such as allyl sulfides, help to combat secondary bacterial infections that can occur during a cold sore outbreak, preventing further complications and promoting recovery.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Allium sativum, also known as garlic, creams helps with cold sore because of its antiviral and antibacterial properties. The active compounds in garlic, such as allicin, have been shown to inhibit the replication of the herpes simplex virus, which causes cold sores. By reducing the viral load and inflammation, garlic creams can help to heal cold sore lesions faster and reduce their severity. Additionally, garlic's antiseptic properties help to prevent bacterial infection and promote a healthy recovery.
remedy_constituents: Allicin: It has antiviral properties that help reduce the severity and duration of cold sore outbreaks by inhibiting the replication of the herpes simplex virus.
Quercetin: This flavonoid antioxidant has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with cold sores.

S-Allyl cysteine sulfoxide (ACS): It has immunomodulatory effects, which may help boost the body's immune response against the herpes simplex virus, thereby reducing the frequency and severity of cold sore outbreaks.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are used because they contain allicin, a compound that has antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce cold sore symptoms.
Leaves: Leaves are used because they have antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent the spread of infection and promote healing.
Flowers: Flowers are used because they contain flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, helping to soothe and calm the skin affected by cold sores.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 200g of allium sativum bulbs and chop them into small pieces for extraction.
Combine the chopped allium sativum with 500ml of carrier oil in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks to allow infusion.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container.
Whip 100g of beeswax and 100g of shea butter with the infused oil in a double boiler.

remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which can help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with cold sores.

Licoricidin: This flavonoid has been shown to inhibit the replication of herpes simplex virus (HSV), which is responsible for causing cold sores, thereby reducing the severity and duration of outbreaks.

Saponins: These natural compounds have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help soothe and calm the skin, reducing irritation and preventing bacterial or fungal infections that can worsen cold sore symptoms.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice, creams helps with cold sore because of its anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. The saponins present in Glycyrrhiza glabra have been shown to reduce redness and swelling associated with cold sores. The cream's moisturizing properties also help to soothe and protect the affected skin, promoting faster healing and reducing the duration of the cold sore outbreak. Additionally, the antibacterial properties of Glycyrrhiza glabra help to prevent secondary infections and promote a healthy recovery.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Help with cold sore by reducing inflammation and acting as an antiviral agent, inhibiting the replication of the herpes simplex virus.
Glycyrrhizin: Assist in soothing and calming the skin, reducing pain and inflammation associated with cold sore outbreaks, while also having antiviral properties.
Flavonoids (especially isorhapontigenin): Exhibit antiviral activity against the herpes simplex virus, inhibiting its replication and reducing the severity of cold sore symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used for their anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties to help soothe and heal cold sore symptoms.
Leaves: Employed for their glycosides content, which has been shown to inhibit the herpes simplex virus, a common cause of cold sores.
Flowers: Utilized for their flavonoids and saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that aid in cold sore recovery.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots and clean them thoroughly to remove any impurities.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 30 minutes.
Mix 20g of the powdered roots with 100g of distilled water in a heat-resistant bowl and heat the mixture over low heat for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture using a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to obtain a clear liquid extract in 10 minutes.
Blend 20g of the extract with 80g of a moisturizing cream base and 2g of preservative in a mixing bowl for 5 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Comfrey contains compounds like allantoin, which helps reduce inflammation and swelling associated with cold sore outbreaks, making it easier to manage discomfort and speed up recovery.

Wound-healing properties: Comfrey's high concentration of allantoin, vitamin B12, and other nutrients promotes wound healing by stimulating collagen production, enhancing tissue repair, and protecting the skin from further damage.

Antiviral properties: Comfrey contains triterpenoid saponins, which have been shown to exhibit antiviral activity against certain viruses, including herpes simplex virus 1 (HSV-1), the primary cause of cold sores.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Symphytum officinale, also known as comfrey, creams helps with cold sore because of its anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. The cream's active compounds, such as allantoin and rosmarinic acid, accelerate wound healing and reduce pain and inflammation associated with cold sores. Additionally, comfrey creams have antimicrobial properties that help prevent bacterial and viral infections from entering the wound, promoting a faster recovery and reducing the risk of scarring. This natural remedy can provide relief and support for individuals suffering from cold sores.
remedy_constituents: Allantoin: Helps to accelerate wound healing and tissue repair by promoting collagen synthesis and enhancing cellular regeneration, which can aid in the recovery from cold sore lesions.

N-Acetylglucosamine (NAG): Inhibits the production of bradykinin, a peptide that contributes to the inflammation and pain associated with cold sores, thereby reducing discomfort and promoting healing.

Rosmarinic acid: Exhibits potent anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which can help reduce the severity and duration of cold sore outbreaks by inhibiting viral replication and modulating the immune response.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used due to their high concentration of allantoin, which helps in healing and soothing cold sore lesions.
Roots: The roots are used for their anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in reducing the pain and discomfort associated with cold sores.
Flowers: The flowers are used for their antiviral properties, which help in combating the herpes simplex virus that causes cold sores.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh symphytum officinale leaves and flowers in the early morning or late afternoon when the plant is in full bloom.
Steam distill the plant material to obtain the essential oil and extract the herbal compounds in 30 minutes.
Combine the essential oil with a carrier oil such as sweet almond oil in a ratio of 1:10.
Blend the herbal extract with a moisturizing base like beeswax and shea butter in a 1:2 ratio.
Stir the mixture for 10 minutes and pour it into glass jars for storage and later use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum creams helps with cold sore because it contains a unique combination of herbal extracts that work synergistically to combat the underlying causes of cold sores. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties reduce redness and swelling, while its antimicrobial agents help eliminate the herpes virus responsible for the outbreak. Additionally, the cream's soothing and moisturizing ingredients provide relief from discomfort and dryness, promoting faster healing and reducing the likelihood of future breakouts.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as eucalyptol and caryophyllene, can reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with cold sore outbreaks.

Antiviral properties: The cream's antiviral compounds, including eugenol and globulol, may help inhibit the replication of the herpes simplex virus (HSV), which causes cold sores, reducing the duration and severity of outbreaks.

Wound-healing properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum's wound-healing compounds, such as polyphenols and flavonoids, can accelerate the healing process by promoting collagen synthesis, improving tissue oxygenation, and enhancing immune response.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in triterpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm cold sore symptoms.
Barks: Contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that exhibit antibacterial and antiviral activity, making them effective against the herpes simplex virus.
Seeds: Packed with omega-6 fatty acids and antioxidants, which can help reduce inflammation and promote tissue repair in cold sore lesions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered eucalyptus globulus with 100ml of coconut oil in a heat-resistant glass beaker, allowing for gentle heat transfer.
Combine the mixture with 20g of beeswax pellets and 10g of vitamin e oil, stirring until the beeswax is fully incorporated and the mixture reaches a temperature of 60°c to 70°c.
Pour the mixture into a lip balm tube or tin, allowing it to cool and solidify at room temperature over a period of two hours.
Once solidified, store the cream in an airtight container at a temperature below 20°c for up to six months, applying a thin layer as needed to affected areas three to five times daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger creams helps with cold sore because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce redness and swelling. The active compound gingerol in ginger also has antiviral properties that work to combat the herpes simplex virus responsible for causing cold sores. Additionally, ginger's warming sensation can promote blood flow to the affected area, speeding up the healing process and reducing pain and discomfort associated with cold sore outbreaks.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerol and shogaol, help reduce swelling and pain associated with cold sores by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Antiviral properties: The antiviral compounds in ginger, including 6-gingerol and 8-gingerol, have been shown to inhibit the replication of herpes simplex virus (HSV), which causes cold sores, reducing the severity and duration of outbreaks.

Analgesic and pain-relieving properties: Ginger's analgesic compounds, such as gingerol and shogaol, help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with cold sores by blocking pain receptors and reducing inflammation.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzome, the underground stem of the ginger plant, contains bioactive compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making it effective in soothing and treating cold sores.

Roots: The roots of the ginger plant contain a high concentration of starch, which is converted into glucose during processing, providing a natural humectant that helps to lock in moisture and soothe dry skin affected by cold sores.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 fresh ginger roots and wash them thoroughly under running water.
Grind the ginger roots into a fine paste using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 250ml of distilled water with 20g of grated beeswax and heat the mixture in a double boiler until melted.
Combine the ginger paste with 30ml of coconut oil and 10ml of jojoba oil then add it to the melted wax mixture stirring well.
Pour the cream mixture into sterilized glass jars and store them in the refrigerator for at least 24 hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric creams helps with cold sore because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The active compound curcumin in turmeric has been shown to reduce the severity and duration of cold sore symptoms by reducing inflammation, pain, and swelling. Additionally, turmeric's antiviral properties help combat the herpes simplex virus that causes cold sores, promoting faster healing and reducing the risk of recurrence. By applying a turmeric cream directly to affected areas, individuals can experience relief from cold sore discomfort and promote a healthier skin environment.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds (curcuminoids): Reduces swelling, pain, and redness associated with cold sores by inhibiting inflammatory enzymes and suppressing immune responses.
Antiviral activity: Inhibits the replication of herpes simplex virus (HSV), which causes cold sores, thereby preventing the spread of the infection and reducing the duration of symptoms.
Wound-healing properties: Accelerates the healing process by promoting collagen synthesis, improving tissue repair, and enhancing immune responses to promote faster resolution of cold sore lesions.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in curcuminoids, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help reduce cold sore symptoms.
Rhyzomes: Contain a high concentration of curcumin, which inhibits herpes simplex virus replication and reduces inflammation associated with cold sores.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality turmeric roots and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix the powdered turmeric with 200ml of distilled water in a heat-proof bowl and stir until it forms a smooth paste.
Add 50g of beeswax, 20g of coconut oil, and 10g of shea butter to the turmeric paste and melt the mixture in a double boiler for 30 minutes.
Remove the mixture from the heat and add 5 drops of tea tree essential oil and 2 tablespoons of aloe vera gel, stirring until well combined.
Pour the mixture into sterilized tubes or jars and let it cool and set at room temperature for 2 hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow creams helps with cold sore because they provide a soothing and protective barrier for the affected area, allowing the skin to heal quickly. The anti-inflammatory properties of marshmallows help reduce redness and swelling, while their moisturizing effects keep the area hydrated and calm. Additionally, the antioxidants in herbal marshmallow creams may help combat the virus that causes cold sores, reducing its severity and duration. By applying a marshmallow cream to the affected area, individuals can experience faster healing and reduced discomfort.
remedy_properties: Mucilages: Marshmallow cream's mucilages, such as polysaccharides and glycoproteins, help to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing inflammation and pain associated with cold sore outbreaks.
Antioxidants: The antioxidant properties of marshmallow creams can help to neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate cold sore symptoms, promoting faster healing and reducing the risk of scarring.

Soothing and anti-inflammatory compounds: Marshmallow cream's soothing agents, such as saponins and flavonoids, have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties that can help to reduce swelling, redness, and discomfort associated with cold sores.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilages, which soothe and calm the skin, making them an effective ingredient in treating cold sore symptoms.
Rhyzomes: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce redness and swelling associated with cold sores.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh marigold flowers and dry them under shade for at least two weeks or until crispy.
Measure 100g of dried marigold flowers and combine with 500ml of cold pressed sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar.
Place the jar in a warm dark place and allow the mixture to steep for 6-8 weeks, shaking occasionally, as the mixture infuses slowly.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, squeezing out as much liquid as possible from the solids.
Mix 10% of the infused oil with 90% of cold cream and add a few drops of vitamin e oil as an antioxidant stabilizer for shelf life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort creams helps with cold sore because it contains hyperforin, a compound that has potent antiviral properties. When applied topically to the affected area, this cream can help reduce the severity and duration of cold sore symptoms. The cream's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant actions also soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and swelling. Additionally, St John's Wort's antimicrobial properties prevent bacterial infection, promoting a faster recovery from the cold sore.
remedy_properties: Hyperforin: This compound has been shown to have antiviral properties, specifically against herpes simplex virus type 1 (HSV-1), which is responsible for causing cold sores. By inhibiting viral replication, hyperforin helps reduce the severity and duration of cold sore outbreaks.

Flavonoids: St. John's Wort creams contain flavonoids, a class of plant compounds that have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Flavonoids can help soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with cold sores.

Tannins: Tannins are another group of plant compounds present in St. John's Wort creams, which possess astringent and anti-inflammatory effects. Tannins can help dry out and constrict the cold sore blister, reducing its size and discomfort, while also preventing bacterial infection.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used for their anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which help reduce redness and itching associated with cold sores.
Flowers: Rich in hyperforin, a compound that has been shown to inhibit the replication of the herpes virus, reducing the severity of cold sore outbreaks.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hypericum perforatum leaves and flowers to obtain sufficient quantity for the recipe.
Combine 20g of dried hypericum perforatum herbs with 100ml of cold-pressed olive oil in a glass jar.
Place the mixture in a dark cupboard for 4 weeks, shaking the jar daily, to allow infusion time.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into another container and discard solids after 30 minutes.
Mix 20g of beeswax with 100ml of the infused oil and heat gently until fully incorporated and smooth.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian creams helps with cold sore because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the affected area, reducing redness and swelling. The cream's moisturizing properties also help to hydrate the skin, speeding up the healing process. Additionally, valerian root is known for its antiviral properties, which can help to combat the herpes simplex virus that causes cold sores, reducing the duration and severity of outbreaks. This natural approach can provide relief from the discomfort and embarrassment associated with cold sores.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory and anti-viral activity: Valerian cream's anti-inflammatory and anti-viral properties help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with cold sores, while also combating the virus that causes them.
Wound healing and tissue repair: Valerian's natural wound-healing properties promote faster recovery from cold sore blisters, reducing scarring and promoting healthy skin regeneration.

Pain relief and analgesic activity: Valerian's analgesic and anti-inflammatory compounds help alleviate itching, burning, and stinging sensations associated with cold sores, providing relief from discomfort and distress.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the valerian plant contain a high concentration of valerenic acid, which has anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, making it effective in reducing cold sore symptoms.
Rhizomes: Valerian rhizomes have been traditionally used to treat various skin conditions, including cold sores, due to their antibacterial and anti-inflammatory compounds.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried valeriana officinalis root extract and grind it into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine 20 grams of the powdered valeriana officinalis root with 100 milliliters of distilled water in a heat-resistant glass bowl.
Simmer the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, to extract the active compounds from the herbal extract.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into another container and discard the solids, then add 200 grams of cold-pressed coconut oil and stir until fully incorporated.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken at room temperature for at least 2 hours before transferring it to an airtight container for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree creams helps with cold sore because they harness the natural antiviral properties of the plant's leaves. The cream absorbs quickly into the skin, directly targeting the HSV-1 virus that causes cold sores. As the virus is neutralized, inflammation and discomfort are reduced, allowing the skin to heal faster and more effectively. Additionally, the cream's antibacterial properties help prevent secondary infections and promote a healthy, pain-free recovery from cold sore outbreaks.
remedy_properties: Adiantum majus extract: This ingredient has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which helps to reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with cold sores.
Glycerin: As a humectant, glycerin attracts and retains moisture in the skin, soothing dryness and cracking caused by cold sore blisters, while also creating a protective barrier against irritants.
Vitamin E: With its antioxidant properties, vitamin E helps to neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate cold sore symptoms, promoting faster healing and reducing scarring.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and swelling associated with cold sores.
Barks: Contain a high concentration of flavonoids, which exhibit antiviral activity against herpes simplex virus, the causative agent of cold sores.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of fresh ginkgo biloba leaves and rinse them gently under running water.
Combine the leaves with 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 24 hours at room temperature allowing the ginkgo to infuse its properties into the oil.
Strain the oil using cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard the solids after 1 hour of pressing.
Mix the filtered oil with 2% beeswax and 0.5% vitamin e oil and stir until the beeswax is fully incorporated.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Slippery elm creams helps with cold sore because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce the pain, swelling, and discomfort associated with cold sore outbreaks. The cream's mucilage, a thick, protective substance, forms a barrier on the skin that helps to lock in moisture and protect the affected area from further irritation. Additionally, slippery elm has natural antibacterial and antiviral properties that may help to combat the underlying causes of the cold sore, promoting faster healing and reducing the risk of scarring or infection.
remedy_properties: Mucilage content: Slippery elm's mucilage property helps to soothe and calm the skin, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with cold sore outbreaks.
Antiviral activity: Some studies have shown that slippery elm contains antiviral compounds that may help prevent the replication of the herpes simplex virus (HSV), which causes cold sores.
Wound healing properties: Slippery elm's mucilage and other compounds may also promote wound healing by reducing inflammation, preventing bacterial growth, and promoting tissue repair.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the slippery elm plant contain mucilages, which provide soothing and calming properties to help reduce inflammation and pain associated with cold sores.
Barks: The barks of the slippery elm plant are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help combat the virus causing cold sores.
remedy_recipe: Harvest ulmus rubra leaves at dawn when dew is still present on the leaves for maximum potency and freshness.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm, well-ventilated area or using a dehydrator at low heat for 6 hours.
Combine 30 grams of dried ulmus rubra leaves with 150 milliliters of carrier oil like coconut or olive oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a dark place at room temperature for 2 weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate infusion and extraction.
Strain the infused liquid through cheesecloth into another container, discard the solids and add 20 grams of beeswax to the remaining liquid, then heat until melted and mixed thoroughly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage creams helps with cold sore because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and redness caused by the virus. The antiviral compounds in sage also inhibit the growth and replication of the herpes simplex virus, allowing the skin to heal faster and reducing the risk of scarring. Additionally, sage's antioxidant properties help soothe and calm the skin, providing relief from discomfort and pain associated with cold sores.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Sage contains compounds like camphor and thujone, which have been shown to inhibit the replication of herpes simplex virus (HSV), the causative agent of cold sores.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage's flavonoids and phenolic acids help reduce swelling, redness, and pain associated with cold sores by suppressing inflammatory responses in the skin.

Antimicrobial properties: Sage's essential oils possess antimicrobial activity against various microorganisms, including bacteria and fungi that can exacerbate or cause secondary infections during a cold sore outbreak.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sage roots contain flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help soothe and calm cold sore symptoms.
Leaves: Sage leaves are rich in antioxidants and terpenes, which can reduce inflammation, combat bacterial and viral infections, and accelerate the healing process of cold sores.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 grams of dried salvia officinalis leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine the powdered herb with 20 grams of beeswax and 30 grams of sweet almond oil in a heat-proof bowl.
Place the mixture in a double boiler and gently warm it over low heat for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully melted.
Remove the bowl from the heat and let it cool to room temperature, then stir in 10 grams of aloe vera gel and 5 drops of tea tree essential oil.
Pour the cooled mixture into sterilized glass jars and store them in the refrigerator for up to 6 months or until needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme creams helps with cold sore because of its antiviral and antibacterial properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as thymol and carvacrol, have been shown to target the HSV-1 virus that causes cold sores, reducing its ability to replicate and spread. Additionally, the cream's anti-inflammatory compounds help to reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with cold sore outbreaks. As a result, herbal thyme creams can provide rapid relief and promote faster healing of cold sores.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Thyme contains compounds like thymol, which have been shown to inhibit the replication of herpes simplex virus (HSV), helping to reduce the severity and duration of cold sore outbreaks.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in thyme possess anti-inflammatory activities, which can help to reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with cold sores.

Antimicrobial properties: Thyme's antimicrobial compounds have been shown to combat secondary bacterial infections that can occur as a result of cold sore outbreaks, promoting faster healing and reducing the risk of infection.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots have antibacterial and antiviral properties, making them effective in reducing the severity and duration of cold sore outbreaks.
Leaves: Thyme leaves contain flavonoids, which help soothe and calm inflamed skin, reducing redness and discomfort associated with cold sores.
remedy_recipe: Infuse dried thyme leaves with olive oil for 2-3 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean glass jar and discard solids.
Combine the strained oil with beeswax and coconut oil in a double boiler on low heat.
Stir the mixture until the beeswax melts and the temperature reaches 160-170°f, taking approximately 10 minutes.
Pour the mixture into sterilized jars and let it cool and solidify at room temperature for 30-40 minutes.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for cold sore are topical treatments made from natural ingredients that help alleviate the symptoms of cold sores, such as pain, inflammation, and discomfort. These creams are formulated with herbs like Melaleuca alternifolia, Aloe vera, Calendula officinalis, Echinacea purpurea, Aloe barbadensis, Hypericum perforatum, Vaccinium myrtillus, Symphytum officinale, Zingiber officinale, and Glycyrrhiza glabra, which have antiviral, anti-inflammatory, and soothing properties. If left untreated, cold sores can cause pain, discomfort, and emotional distress, affecting daily activities and relationships, making it difficult to manage work, social life, and personal hygiene.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to cold sore that are treated with herbal creams are fever blisters, canker sores, and herpes simplex. These conditions cause painful lesions on the skin or mucous membranes, often triggered by stress, sun exposure, or hormonal fluctuations. Herbal creams containing ingredients like aloe vera, tea tree oil, and calendula can help soothe and calm these symptoms, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with cold sore is a blend of Aloe vera, Tea tree oil, and Echinacea. Aloe vera soothes and calms the skin, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. Tea tree oil has antiviral and antibacterial properties that help combat the herpes simplex virus, while Echinacea boosts the immune system and accelerates recovery. Applying these creams topically, ideally in combination with a healthy diet and lifestyle, can help alleviate cold sore symptoms and promote faster healing. This natural approach can provide relief and reduce the frequency of recurrences.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia creams helps with cold sore because they contain tea tree oil, a natural antiviral and antibacterial agent. This oil reduces inflammation and promotes healing, making it an effective treatment for cold sore outbreaks. The antimicrobial properties of tea tree oil also help to prevent the spread of the herpes simplex virus, which causes cold sores. By applying a Melaleuca alternifolia cream to the affected area, individuals can help to alleviate symptoms and reduce the duration of a cold sore outbreak.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): Helps with cold sore by reducing inflammation and relieving pain due to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Linalool: Contributes to cold sore relief by inhibiting the replication of herpes simplex virus and reducing inflammation and pain.
Methyleugenol: Helps with cold sore by exhibiting antiviral properties, which can reduce the severity and duration of cold sore outbreaks.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The primary part used to make creams for cold sores, as they contain tea tree oil which has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that help combat the herpes simplex virus.
Rhyzomes: The secondary part used to make creams for cold sores, as they also contain tea tree oil that complements the oil extracted from the leaves, enhancing the overall effect of the cream.

Roots: The third part used to make creams for cold sores, as they contain additional compounds that enhance the antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties of the tea tree oil, further aiding in the treatment of cold sores.
remedy_recipe: Gather 250 grams of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil and 15 grams of dried melaleuca alternifolia leaves.
Infuse the carrier oil with the dried melaleuca alternifolia leaves for 2 hours in a double boiler.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean container to remove the solids.
Mix 1 teaspoon of beeswax and 1 teaspoon of shea butter into the infused oil to thicken it.
Whip the mixture with an electric mixer for 5 minutes until it becomes a smooth and creamy texture.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera creams helps with cold sore because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The gel extracted from the Aloe vera plant contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which have been shown to reduce redness and swelling associated with cold sore outbreaks. Additionally, Aloe vera's antimicrobial properties help combat the herpes simplex virus that causes cold sores. These properties work together to provide relief from the pain, itching, and discomfort caused by cold sores, helping to speed up the healing process.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: These constituents have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which help to reduce the severity and duration of cold sore outbreaks.
Saponins: Saponins exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities, which can help to combat the herpes simplex virus that causes cold sores and alleviate symptoms such as pain and redness.

Glycoproteins: Glycoproteins in Aloe vera have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which can help to regulate the body's immune response and reduce the risk of cold sore recurrences.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The gel extracted from the leaves is used due to its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties.
Seeds: The oil extracted from the seeds is used for its antiviral properties, which can help combat the herpes simplex virus that causes cold sores.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 6 to 8 ounces of pure aloe vera gel from the leaf.
Mix 2 tablespoons of coconut oil with 2 tablespoons of shea butter in a bowl.
Add 2 teaspoons of vitamin e oil to the coconut oil and shea butter mixture.
Combine 2 tablespoons of beeswax with 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Blend the aloe vera gel with the beeswax mixture until it reaches a smooth consistency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis creams helps with cold sore because it possesses anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in reducing the severity and duration of the outbreak. The cream's ability to soothe and calm the skin can also help to reduce discomfort and promote faster healing. Additionally, calendula's antioxidant properties can help to combat the underlying viral cause of cold sores, making it a popular natural remedy for this common condition. Its effectiveness in reducing symptoms makes it a valuable treatment option.
remedy_constituents: Carotenoids: These compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce the severity and duration of cold sore outbreaks.
Flavonoids: They have potent antiviral and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help inhibit the replication of the herpes simplex virus responsible for cold sores and reduce inflammation and pain.
Triterpenoids: These compounds possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and protect the affected area, reducing the risk of infection and promoting healing.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are rich in anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds, such as triterpene saponins, which help to soothe and heal cold sore lesions.
Leaves: They contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, making them useful in treating cold sore symptoms.

Stems: Although less commonly used, some herbal remedies may incorporate calendula stems to take advantage of their secondary metabolites, which contribute to the plant's overall medicinal properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried calendula flowers at the peak of their potency and store them in airtight containers.
Combine 100g of beeswax with 100g of coconut oil and 50g of olive oil in a double boiler.
Add the 100g of dried calendula flowers to the oil mixture in the double boiler and heat gently.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it infuse for 2 hours or overnight before straining it.
Whisk 200g of shea butter with the strained mixture and pour it into tin containers for 20 minutes to set.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams helps with cold sore because of its anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and pain associated with the outbreak. The cream's immunomodulatory effects also help to stimulate the body's immune response, promoting the healing of the cold sore. Additionally, Echinacea purpurea contains compounds that have antiviral properties, which can help to inhibit the replication of the herpes simplex virus, the underlying cause of cold sores. This results in a faster recovery and reduced frequency of outbreaks.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the swelling and pain associated with cold sores.
Triterpene Glycosides: They exhibit antiviral and immune-modulating activities, which can help combat the herpes simplex virus that causes cold sores and boost the body's natural defenses.
Flavonoids: These compounds possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the severity and duration of cold sore outbreaks and promote healing.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Used due to their high content of alkylamides and phenolic compounds, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
Roots: Used due to their rich concentration of glycosides, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds that help combat cold sore viruses.
Leaves: Used due to their content of flavonoids and phenolic compounds, which may contribute to their potential antiviral and anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/2 cup of dried echinacea purpurea flowers and 1/4 cup of dried echinacea roots.
Infuse the herbs in 2 cups of boiling water for 10-15 minutes then strain the mixture.
Combine 1/2 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Add 2 tablespoons of the infused herbal mixture and 2 tablespoons of shea butter to the melted wax and oil mixture.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and solidify for 30-40 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams helps with cold sore because of its potent anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The gel extracted from the Aloe vera plant contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and accelerate the healing process of cold sore lesions. Additionally, Aloe vera's antimicrobial properties help prevent secondary infections, promoting a faster recovery and reducing the risk of scarring. Its moisturizing properties also aid in skin regeneration, leaving the affected area smooth and supple.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which help to reduce the severity and duration of cold sore outbreaks.
Saponins: These compounds have antiviral and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce the replication of the herpes simplex virus that causes cold sores.
Polysaccharides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which can help to reduce the severity of cold sore symptoms and promote healing of the skin.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high content of aloe vera gel, which has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help to reduce cold sore pain and promote healing.
Leaves: Used for their ability to stimulate collagen production, which helps to reduce the appearance of cold sore scars and promote skin elasticity.
Leaves: Used due to their antioxidant properties, which help to protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals and promote overall skin health.
remedy_recipe: Extract the aloe barbadensis gel by scooping out the clear inner portion of 5-7 inch aloe vera leaves after 30 minutes.
Mix 2 tablespoons of the extracted aloe gel with 1 teaspoon of distilled water to create a thin consistency in 5 minutes.
Combine the aloe gel mixture with 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in 5 minutes.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 150-170 degrees fahrenheit for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl and let it cool to room temperature in 20 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum creams helps with cold sore because they contain St. John's Wort, a natural anti-inflammatory and antiviral agent. The active compounds in Hypericum perforatum, such as hyperforin and hypericin, have been shown to reduce inflammation and viral replication associated with cold sore outbreaks. Additionally, Hypericum perforatum creams have antimicrobial properties that help combat secondary infections, promoting faster healing and reducing the duration of cold sore episodes. This natural approach can provide relief and support the body's recovery process.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: Exhibits anti-viral properties, which help inhibit the replication of the herpes simplex virus responsible for cold sores.
Naphthodianthrones (e.g., hypericin and pseudohypericin): Display anti-viral and anti-inflammatory activities, which aid in reducing the severity and duration of cold sore outbreaks.
Flavonoids (e.g., quercetin and kaempferol): Possess anti-inflammatory and anti-oxidant properties, which help soothe and calm the skin, reducing pain and inflammation associated with cold sores.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Hypericum perforatum are commonly used because they contain flavonoids, such as hyperforin and hypericin, which have antiviral properties.
Flowers: The flowers are used due to their high concentration of flavonoids, which are effective in reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing.
Buds: The buds of Hypericum perforatum are used as they contain a high amount of hyperforin, a compound that has antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the hypericum perforatum flowers and leaves in the morning when the dew is still present on them for better quality.
Dry the harvested plant material using a dehydrator at 40 degrees celsius for 6 hours to preserve its properties.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mill.
Mix the powdered plant material with 20 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of coconut oil to create a paste.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 60 degrees celsius for 30 minutes to create a smooth cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Vaccinium myrtillus creams helps with cold sore because they contain compounds found in bilberry, a natural antiviral agent that targets the herpes simplex virus, which causes cold sores. The astringent properties of Vaccinium myrtillus can also help reduce inflammation and prevent the spread of the virus. Additionally, its antioxidant properties may aid in healing the skin and reducing the severity and duration of cold sore outbreaks, providing quick relief and promoting faster recovery. This makes it a popular natural remedy for cold sore sufferers.
remedy_constituents: Anthocyanins: These powerful antioxidants help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which are common triggers for cold sore outbreaks.
Quercetin: This flavonoid has potent anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which help alleviate symptoms and reduce the severity of cold sores.

Phenolic acids (e.g., chlorogenic acid): These compounds exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities, which can help prevent the spread of the herpes simplex virus and reduce cold sore symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Extracts from the leaves are used due to their antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate cold sore symptoms.
Fruits: The fruits, also known as blueberries, are used because they contain anthocyanins and other compounds that exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects.
Barks: The barks of Vaccinium myrtillus contain flavonoids and other compounds with antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to the effectiveness of cold sore creams.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250g of vaccinium myrtillus berries in the early morning to ensure maximum potency and freshness.
Steam distill the harvested berries in a large pot for 45 minutes to release their essential oils.
Combine 10ml of the distilled essential oils with 50g of beeswax and 50g of shea butter in a heat-proof bowl.
Melt the beeswax and shea butter mixture over low heat for 20 minutes while stirring constantly with a spatula.
Pour the melted mixture into small tin containers and allow it to cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Symphytum officinale creams helps with cold sore because they contain comfrey, a natural anti-inflammatory agent that reduces swelling and discomfort. The cream's antiviral properties also help combat the herpes simplex virus, which causes cold sores. Additionally, comfrey's wound-healing properties promote faster recovery, reducing the duration and severity of cold sores. Its soothing and protective action on the skin also helps to prevent further irritation, promoting a speedy and effective healing process. This natural remedy provides relief and promotes a healthy recovery from cold sores.
remedy_constituents: Alkaloids: These compounds help to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with cold sores by blocking the release of pain-causing chemicals.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids, particularly kaempferol and quercetin, exhibit anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which help to combat the herpes simplex virus that causes cold sores.
Saponins: Saponins have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to soothe and protect the affected skin, reducing the severity and duration of cold sore outbreaks.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of allantoin, which helps to accelerate wound healing.
Roots: The roots are used due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce pain and swelling associated with cold sores.
Buds: The buds are used due to their high concentration of mucilage, a soothing agent that helps to reduce inflammation and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 grams of dried symphytum officinale roots and leaves in late spring or early summer for optimal potency.
Infuse 10 grams of the harvested symphytum officinale in 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container to remove excess plant material.
Mix 20 grams of beeswax with 20 milliliters of the infused oil in a double boiler to create a smooth consistency.
Allow the mixture to cool and solidify completely before transferring it to a container for storage and use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams helps with cold sore because of its anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. The active ingredient in ginger, zingibain, has been shown to reduce pain and inflammation associated with cold sores. The cream's ability to inhibit the herpes simplex virus also helps to shorten the duration and severity of the outbreak. By reducing inflammation and viral activity, Zingiber officinale creams provide relief from cold sore symptoms, such as redness, swelling, and discomfort, allowing for faster healing and reduced risk of recurrence.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: Have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and inflammation associated with cold sores.
Zingiberene: Exhibits antiviral activity that helps combat the herpes simplex virus, the primary cause of cold sores.
Shogaols: Show antimicrobial properties, which help prevent the spread of infection and reduce the severity of cold sore symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the plant are used in some formulations due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can aid in soothing cold sore symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Combine 2 cups of distilled water and 1 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Steep 1/4 cup of dried zingiber officinale root in the distilled water mixture for 20-30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the solids from the liquid.
Add 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 1 teaspoon of vitamin e oil to the liquid mixture.
Whisk the mixture until the beeswax is fully incorporated and the cream is smooth and creamy.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams helps with cold sore because of its anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. The licorice root extract in these creams has been shown to reduce swelling and redness associated with cold sores. Additionally, Glycyrrhiza glabra contains flavonoids and glycosides that have been found to inhibit the replication of the herpes simplex virus, the primary cause of cold sores. By reducing inflammation and inhibiting viral replication, Glycyrrhiza glabra creams can help alleviate symptoms and promote faster healing of cold sores.
remedy_constituents: Licopyrones (Glycyrrhizin): This triterpenoid saponin has anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which help to reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with cold sores.

Flavonoids (Isoliquiritin and Liquiritin): As antiviral agents, these flavonoids help to inhibit the replication of herpes simplex virus, thereby reducing the severity and duration of cold sore outbreaks.

Phenolic acids (Glycosides): These phenolic acids exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to protect the skin from oxidative stress and reduce the formation of cold sore lesions.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used to make creams for cold sores due to their rich content of glycyrrhizin, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms.
Leaves: The leaves are used to make creams for cold sores due to their high glycyrrhizin content, which can aid in reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
Barks: The barks are used to make creams for cold sores due to their ability to soothe and calm irritated skin, providing relief from cold sore symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Grate 100g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots into fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the glycyrrhiza glabra powder with 20ml of distilled water in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10 minutes to create a decoction.
Mix 20g of beeswax and 10g of coconut oil with the decoction in a separate bowl.
Whip the mixture until it thickens and forms a smooth creamy texture.
